bool inverted

SurfaceMesh * mesh
 Mesh that contains the triangle and its edge.
 
int side
 Triangle side used at construction (FRONT or BACK)
 

Detailed Description

RWG basis function tied to a mesh triangle.

Given a triangle and one of its vertices designated as the "free" vertex, the RWG function is (up to scale) linear along the triangle and proportional to (r − r_free) (or the negative thereof) depending on orientation.

This class offers:

  • Construction from mesh, triangle, free vertex and side (FRONT/BACK).
  • Pointwise evaluation of the vector shape function on the triangle.
  • Accessors for triangle/edge bookkeeping and sign/orientation.

Definition at line 30 of file RWGFun.h.

Constructor & Destructor Documentation

◆ RWGFun()

RWGFun::RWGFun ( SurfaceMesh * inMesh,
Triangle * inTri,
rvec * inFreeVertex,
int inSide )

Construct an RWG basis function tied to one triangle.

Determines the opposite edge from inFreeVertex, resolves boundary conditions (periodic/symmetry) via SurfaceMesh helpers if needed, and sets the orientation (inverted) based on inSide and geometry.

Parameters
inMeshOwning mesh.
inTriPointer to the triangle supporting this half-RWG.
inFreeVertexPointer to the free vertex of the RWG on inTri.
inSideFRONT or BACK (affects sign/orientation).

Locates the edge opposite to inFreeVertex, handles boundary-condition edges (periodic/symmetry) by mapping and augmenting the mesh as needed, and determines the sign (inverted) based on the requested inSide and the geometric orientation between edge/normal/free-vertex.

Definition at line 118 of file RWGFun.cpp.

◆ Evaluate()

rvec RWGFun::Evaluate ( rvec r)

Evaluate the RWG vector shape function at a point on the triangle.

Returns \(\pm\)(r − r_free) * L_edge / (2 * A_triangle), where the sign depends on orientation (inverted flag). The caller must ensure r lies on the triangle; no membership check is performed here.

Parameters
rPoint of evaluation (in the triangle's plane).
Returns
RWG vector value at r.

Evaluate the RWG vector function at point r on the triangle.

Uses the standard half-RWG formula: f(r) = \(\pm\) (r − r_free) * (L_edge) / (2 A_tri), where the sign is controlled by Inverted().

◆ RWGPreFactor()

double RWGFun::RWGPreFactor ( )

Return \(\pm\)L_n / A_n (missing factor 1/2 by convention).

Returns
RWG pre-factor.

This equals the surface divergence of the RWG function on the triangle. Sign depends on Inverted().

Return \(\pm\)L_n / A_n (missing factor 1/2), equal to surface divergence. Sign reflects Inverted().
